strengths: Demonstrated excellent bimanual coordination with both instruments moving simultaneously
Achieved strong completion time of 38 seconds, well below the FLS benchmark
Maintained consistent peg transfer pattern throughout the sequence
Showed proper edge-gripping technique for secure peg handling
weaknesses: Occasional single-handed operation visible in early phases
Some hesitation during initial pickup and positioning phases
Field of view could be optimized for more efficient instrument coordination

drills.name: Simultaneous motion ladder
drills.description: Practice 10 transfers where both instruments must be moving at all times, pausing only for secure grips.
drills.targets_weakness: Occasional single-handed operation visible in early phases.

drills.name: Camera centering reset
drills.description: 3 minutes of centering and re-centering the work target before the first move to optimize field of view.
drills.targets_weakness: Field of view could be optimized for more efficient instrument coordination.

drills.name: First-move confidence drill
drills.description: Practice the initial pickup sequence 15 times with deliberate, confident motion to eliminate hesitation.
drills.targets_weakness: Some hesitation during initial pickup and positioning phases.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your solid fundamentals into consistently fluid bimanual motion to approach the expert benchmark of under 30 seconds.
